source_rationale: CXCL12 (SDF-1) is a chemokine highly expressed by bone marrow stromal cells that supports hematopoietic stem cell maintenance
Leptin receptor marks a major population of bone marrow stromal/mesenchymal progenitor cells
SCF/KITLG is produced by bone marrow stromal cells and is essential for hematopoietic niche function
CD73 (NT5E) is a mesenchymal stromal cell surface marker used in ISCT criteria for MSC identification
CD90 (THY1) is a canonical surface marker for mesenchymal stromal cells including bone marrow-derived MSCs
CD105 (ENG) is a standard ISCT marker for mesenchymal stromal cells
VCAM1 is expressed on bone marrow stromal cells and mediates interactions with hematopoietic progenitors
PDGFRα marks mesenchymal progenitors in the bone marrow stroma
SOX9 is the master transcription factor for chondrogenesis, highly expressed in chondroblasts
Type II collagen is the primary collagen produced during early chondrogenesis by chondroblasts
Aggrecan is a major cartilage proteoglycan expressed by differentiating chondroblasts
SOX5 cooperates with SOX9 to drive chondroblast differentiation and cartilage matrix gene expression
SOX6 works with SOX5 and SOX9 as part of the chondrogenic SOX trio in chondroblasts
Type XI collagen is expressed during early cartilage formation by chondroblasts
Matrilin-1 is an extracellular matrix protein expressed in developing cartilage by chondroblasts
Type II collagen is the hallmark extracellular matrix protein of mature chondrocytes
Aggrecan is the major proteoglycan of cartilage, abundantly produced by chondrocytes
SOX9 is the master chondrogenic transcription factor maintained in mature chondrocytes
Type IX collagen is a cartilage-specific collagen expressed by chondrocytes
Type XI collagen is a minor cartilage collagen specifically expressed by chondrocytes
Cartilage oligomeric matrix protein is a non-collagenous ECM component specific to chondrocytes
Type X collagen marks hypertrophic chondrocytes during endochondral ossification
Matrilin-3 is a cartilage extracellular matrix protein expressed by chondrocytes
GDF5 is expressed in joint-forming chondrocytes and plays a role in cartilage homeostasis
SOX10 is a key transcription factor expressed in neural crest cells, essential for their specification and migration
FOXD3 is a transcription factor critical for neural crest cell specification and maintenance of multipotency
TFAP2A is a transcription factor essential for cranial neural crest cell induction and development
PAX3 is expressed in premigratory and early migratory neural crest cells
PAX7 marks neural crest progenitors, particularly in the cranial region
SLUG/SNAI2 is a transcription factor involved in neural crest cell epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition
DLX2 is specifically expressed in cranial neural crest cells contributing to craniofacial structures
TWIST1 is expressed in cranial neural crest cells and is essential for craniofacial development
SOX9 is essential for skeletal progenitor specification and early chondro-osteogenic commitment
PRRX1 marks early limb mesenchymal progenitors that give rise to skeletal elements
RUNX2 is an early osteogenic transcription factor expressed in skeletal progenitors
Type II collagen is expressed in early skeletal condensations by skeletal progenitors
PDGFRα marks mesenchymal progenitors including embryonic skeletal stem cells
GDF5 marks skeletal progenitors particularly at joint interzone regions
BMP receptor 1B is expressed in skeletal progenitors and mediates chondrogenic signaling
CD90/THY1 is expressed on mesenchymal/skeletal progenitor cells
CD31/PECAM1 is a canonical endothelial cell surface marker expressed at cell-cell junctions
VE-cadherin is an endothelial-specific adherens junction protein
VEGFR2/KDR is the primary VEGF receptor expressed on endothelial cells
VEGFR1/FLT1 is a VEGF receptor expressed on endothelial cells
Von Willebrand factor is produced by endothelial cells and is a classic endothelial marker
ERG is an ETS family transcription factor constitutively expressed in endothelial cells
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein enriched in endothelial cells
Endomucin is a sialomucin expressed on the luminal surface of endothelial cells
EpCAM is a canonical epithelial cell adhesion molecule broadly expressed on epithelial cells
Keratin 18 is a type I intermediate filament expressed in simple epithelial cells
Keratin 8 is a type II intermediate filament characteristic of simple epithelia
E-cadherin is a hallmark epithelial adhesion molecule
Keratin 19 is expressed in various epithelial cell types
Claudin-4 is a tight junction protein expressed in epithelial cells
EpCAM is broadly expressed on epithelial cells of various subtypes
Keratin 14 is a basal epithelial cell marker expressed in stratified epithelia
Keratin 5 is a basal keratin expressed in stratified epithelial cells
p63 is a transcription factor marking basal/stratified epithelial cells
E-cadherin is a fundamental epithelial cell adhesion molecule
Keratin 17 is expressed in basal and myoepithelial cells of stratified epithelia
Hemoglobin alpha 1 is a major component of adult hemoglobin in erythrocytes
Hemoglobin alpha 2 is a component of hemoglobin abundantly expressed in erythrocytes
Hemoglobin beta is the major beta-globin gene expressed in mature erythrocytes
Glycophorin A is a major sialoglycoprotein on the erythrocyte surface
Band 3 (SLC4A1) is the major anion exchanger on the erythrocyte membrane
ALAS2 is the erythroid-specific aminolevulinate synthase essential for heme biosynthesis
Ankyrin-1 is a key erythrocyte cytoskeletal protein
GATA1 is the master transcription factor for erythroid lineage differentiation
KLF1 (EKLF) is an erythroid-specific transcription factor essential for beta-globin expression
PRRX1 is a homeobox gene broadly expressed in limb bud mesenchyme
TBX5 is a T-box transcription factor expressed in forelimb bud mesenchyme
PITX1 is expressed in hindlimb bud mesenchyme and specifies hindlimb identity
HAND2 is expressed in posterior limb bud mesenchyme
HOXA13 is expressed in distal limb bud mesenchyme and patterns autopod development
FGF10 is expressed in limb bud mesenchyme and signals to the overlying ectoderm
PDGFRα is broadly expressed in limb mesenchymal progenitor cells
PRRX1 is a limb mesenchyme marker expressed in undifferentiated limb bud cells
HOXD13 is expressed in distal limb mesenchyme and patterns digit formation
MSX1 is expressed in limb bud mesenchyme, particularly in the progress zone
MSX2 is expressed in limb mesenchyme and involved in limb patterning
LHX2 is expressed in limb bud mesenchyme during early limb development
IRX3 is expressed in limb mesenchyme and involved in dorsoventral patterning
PRRX1 is broadly expressed in limb bud mesenchymal populations
HOXA11 marks zeugopod (middle limb segment) mesenchymal cells
SHOX2 is expressed in proximal limb mesenchyme and involved in limb segment specification
TBX3 is expressed in limb mesenchyme and involved in anterior-posterior patterning
PDGFRα marks mesenchymal progenitors in the developing limb
Type I collagen is expressed by mesenchymal cells in the developing limb
PRRX1 is a general limb mesenchyme transcription factor
SHH is expressed in the zone of polarizing activity of the posterior limb bud mesenchyme
HOXD12 is expressed in posterior-distal limb mesenchyme
PRRX2 is expressed in limb mesenchyme and involved in connective tissue differentiation
ALX4 is expressed in limb mesenchyme and involved in limb patterning
OSR1 is expressed in limb mesenchyme and marks connective tissue progenitors
CD68 is a lysosomal glycoprotein highly expressed in macrophages and monocytes
CD163 is a hemoglobin scavenger receptor that serves as a specific marker for tissue-resident macrophages
CSF1R (CD115) is the M-CSF receptor essential for macrophage differentiation and survival
MARCO is a scavenger receptor expressed on macrophages involved in pathogen recognition
CD206/MRC1 is the mannose receptor expressed on tissue macrophages
ADGRE1 (EMR1) is an adhesion GPCR expressed on macrophages
IBA1/AIF1 is a calcium-binding protein expressed in macrophages and microglia
CD45/PTPRC is a pan-leukocyte marker expressed on all macrophages
Complement C1q subunit A is highly expressed in tissue-resident macrophages
SOX10 is maintained in migratory neural crest cells and essential for their differentiation
SNAIL/SNAI1 drives epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition enabling neural crest cell migration
SLUG/SNAI2 is critical for neural crest EMT and active migration
TWIST1 promotes mesenchymal properties in migratory cranial neural crest cells
ETS1 is expressed in migratory neural crest cells and regulates migration-related genes
TFAP2A is maintained during cranial neural crest migration and regulates target genes
Neuropilin-1 mediates guidance signaling in migratory neural crest cells
CRABP1 is expressed in migratory neural crest cells and modulates retinoic acid signaling
DLX2 is expressed in migratory cranial neural crest cells destined for branchial arch structures
MYOD1 is a master myogenic regulatory factor that commits cells to the skeletal muscle lineage
MYF5 is an early myogenic regulatory factor expressed in proliferating myoblasts
PAX7 marks satellite cells and proliferating myoblasts
PAX3 is expressed in myogenic progenitors and early myoblasts during embryonic development
Desmin is an intermediate filament protein expressed in committed myoblasts
NCAM1/CD56 is a surface marker used to isolate myoblasts from muscle tissue
Myogenin is expressed in myoblasts undergoing terminal differentiation
Embryonic myosin heavy chain is expressed in differentiating/embryonic myocytes
Skeletal muscle alpha-actin is a major contractile protein in differentiated myocytes
Titin is a giant sarcomeric protein essential for myocyte structure and function
Myogenin drives terminal differentiation of myocytes
Myosin light chain 1 is expressed in differentiated skeletal myocytes
Muscle creatine kinase is highly expressed in differentiated skeletal myocytes
Slow skeletal troponin T is expressed in differentiated myocytes
Desmin is an intermediate filament protein abundantly expressed in mature myocytes
Perinatal myosin heavy chain is expressed in developing myocytes
PAX3 is a key transcription factor in early myogenic progenitors during embryonic development
PAX7 marks myogenic progenitors and satellite cells
MYF5 is expressed in early myogenic progenitors committed to the muscle lineage
SIX1 is a transcription factor upstream of myogenic regulatory factors in muscle progenitors
SIX4 cooperates with SIX1 in activating myogenic gene expression in progenitors
MEOX1 is expressed in somitic mesoderm-derived myogenic progenitors
PITX2 regulates myogenic progenitor proliferation during muscle development
SOX10 is a hallmark transcription factor of neural crest-derived lineages
FOXD3 is expressed in neural crest-derived cells and maintains their progenitor state
TFAP2A is a transcription factor broadly expressed in neural crest-derived cells
p75NTR/NGFR is a surface marker expressed on neural crest-derived cells
Nestin is expressed in neural crest-derived progenitor cells
ERBB3 is expressed in neural crest-derived cells including Schwann cell precursors
SOX9 is expressed in neural crest-derived mesenchymal cells contributing to craniofacial structures
SOX9 is the master regulator of chondrogenesis expressed in osteo-chondrogenic progenitors
RUNX2 is the master osteogenic transcription factor co-expressed with SOX9 in bipotent progenitors
Type II collagen is expressed in progenitors committing to the chondrogenic lineage
Type I collagen is expressed in progenitors with osteogenic potential
PRRX1 marks mesenchymal progenitors with osteo-chondrogenic differentiation capacity
OSR2 is expressed in mesenchymal progenitors contributing to skeletal elements
TWIST1 is expressed in osteo-chondrogenic progenitors and regulates lineage commitment
PDGFRα marks mesenchymal progenitors with skeletal differentiation potential
RUNX2 is the master transcription factor for osteoblast differentiation expressed in osteoprogenitors
Osterix/SP7 is a zinc-finger transcription factor essential for osteoblast differentiation downstream of RUNX2
Type I collagen is the major organic component of bone matrix produced by osteogenic cells
Alkaline phosphatase is an early osteoblast marker expressed in osteoprogenitors
Osteopontin is expressed by osteoprogenitors and osteoblasts during bone formation
Bone sialoprotein is expressed during osteoblast differentiation
DLX5 is a transcription factor that promotes osteoblast differentiation
Type I collagen alpha-2 chain is abundantly expressed in osteogenic lineage cells
Type I collagen is highly expressed in perichondrial cells surrounding cartilage
Periostin is expressed in perichondrial/periosteal mesenchymal cells
PDGFRα marks mesenchymal stromal cells in the perichondrium
CD90/THY1 is expressed on perichondrial mesenchymal stromal cells
TWIST1 is expressed in perichondrial mesenchyme and regulates skeletal development
Type III collagen is expressed by perichondrial stromal cells
Decorin is a proteoglycan expressed in perichondrial mesenchymal cells
Type I collagen is a major product of perichondrial stromal cells
Periostin is enriched in perichondrial/periosteal cells
RUNX2 expression in perichondrial cells indicates osteogenic potential
Type III collagen is expressed in perichondrial connective tissue
PDGFRα marks mesenchymal progenitors in the perichondrium
Fibronectin is expressed by perichondrial stromal cells as part of the extracellular matrix
Type I collagen is expressed by perichondrial mesenchymal cells
SOX9 expression in perichondrial cells indicates chondrogenic potential
PTH/PTHrP receptor is expressed in perichondrial cells and regulates chondrocyte differentiation
FGFR3 is expressed in perichondrial cells and regulates cartilage growth
Type I collagen alpha-2 is expressed in perichondrial stromal cells
Decorin is a small leucine-rich proteoglycan expressed in perichondrial mesenchyme
PDGFRβ is a canonical pericyte marker essential for pericyte recruitment to blood vessels
RGS5 is a regulator of G-protein signaling highly expressed in pericytes

NG2/CSPG4 is a proteoglycan expressed on the surface of pericytes

SOX10 is essential for Schwann cell development and maintained in mature Schwann cells
